title: helichrysum purdiei
intro: Helichrysum purdiei is a flowering plant species within the Helichrysum genus, commonly known as a daisy bush or yellow buttons, native to the southwestern and southern regions of Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, injuries, and inflammatory conditions, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Helichrysum purdiei include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and alleviating pain, as well as possessing antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or essential oil, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
